items.title: Backpropagation in neural networks
items.description: Training computes ∂L/∂θ by chaining local derivatives from the loss backward through every layer and activation.

items.title: Automatic differentiation in ML frameworks
items.description: PyTorch, JAX and TensorFlow record ops and apply the chain rule on primitives so users need not hand-derive gradients for complex models.

items.title: Composed loss functions
items.description: Losses like cross-entropy with softmax, or perceptual losses through a frozen encoder, require the chain rule through each sub-block.

items.title: Fine-tuning pretrained models
items.description: Gradients flow through the full forward graph—including frozen and trainable parts—via the same chain-rule propagation during backward passes.
